title: Anthropic strikes SpaceX data centre deal as it plows ahead on AI coding
description: Anthropic on Wednesday said it reached a deal to tap ‌the computing resources of Elon Musk’s SpaceX, marking a détente with its one-time critic and a boost ​for both companies in the high-stakes artificial intelligence race.

title: Data centre IPOs set to attract billions as investors line up AI bets
description: Wall Street banks are getting ready to raise billions of dollars taking data centre companies public, even after IPO investors have already piled into anything that looks like a bet on artificial intelligence (AI) spending.
content: The initial public offering for a Blackstone data-centre acquisition vehicle next week is set to fire the starting gun on a host of activity over the coming 18 months, and together with Singapore-based DayOne Data Centres may raise close to $7bn (€5.... [3456 chars]

title: Worries about AI's risks to humanity loom over Elon Musk's trial against OpenAI
description: Questions about the dangers of artificial intelligence have been looming over the trial pitting Elon Musk against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman.
content:
OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — At the heart of the trial pitting Elon Musk against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is a moment when they found common cause on an ever more pressing question: how to protect humanity from the risks of artificial intelligence.

title: Mythos Shows AI Weapons Inspectors Need Sharp Teeth
description: Here’s something you don’t see every day: In the midst of an urgent global race to deploy artificial intelligence, some tech companies are voluntarily reining themselves in. Microsoft Corp., Alphabet Inc. and Elon Musk’s xAI have agreed to give US officials early access to new ​AI models, for national security testing. The White House has meanwhile dropped its light-touch approach to the booming business and is exploring ways to set standards.

title: EU countries, lawmakers clinch provisional deal on watered-down AI rules
description: EU countries and European Parliament lawmakers on Thursday agreed to watered-down landmark artificial ‌intelligence rules, including delaying their implementation, in a move critics say shows Europe caving in to Big Tech.
content:
BRUSSELS, May 7 (Reuters) - EU countries and European Parliament lawmakers on Thursday agreed to watered-down landmark artificial ‌intelligence rules, including delaying their implementation, in a move critics say shows Europe caving in to Big Tech.

title: EU Nations and Parliament Reach AI Rule Compromise
description: EU countries and European Parliament have agreed to a softened version of proposed AI regulations and postponed their activation. The move comes after lobbying by certain governments and corporations for more flexibility in the rules governing artificial intelligence across European nations.
content: European Union member states and lawmakers from the European Parliament have struck an accord to revise landmark regulations concerning artificial intelligence. This decision, which includes a postponement of the rules' implementation, follows signif... [611 chars]

title: US, China weigh launch of official discussions on AI: WSJ
description: Washington and Beijing are reportedly considering official discussions on artificial intelligence. This topic may be placed on the agenda for an upcoming summit between President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing next week. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is leading the American side for these potential AI talks.

title: Hormuz crisis shows gaps in Taiwan’s high-tech ‘silicon shield’
description: The factors that make Taiwan an economic powerhouse — its successful technology industry and artificial intelligence-driven chipmaking — are also the ones making it vulnerable.
content:
The breakwater stretches out to sea from the sprawling Kaohsiung port in southern Taiwan. Normally, it’s crowded with massive tankers ferrying liquefied natural gas from Qatar to be stored in the bulbous white tanks that dot the shoreline.
